occurrence_sentence: Will Africa miss the next computational revolution?

[The National Institute for Theoretical Physics (NITheP)](https://twitter.com/nithepsa?lang=en) has had over 30 summer schools in South Africa, exposing students to theoretical science. For the first time, they partnered with the [Centre for High Performance Computing (CHPC)](https://www.chpc.ac.za/) in 2019 and included streams on quantum computing and computational science.

The [second joint school in 2020](https://quantum.ukzn.ac.za/2020-nithep-chpc-summer-school/) proved to be just as successful with a diverse audience and an even bigger focus on quantum computing with speakers from leading international organisations.

The National Institute for Theoretical Physics (NITheP) has had over 30 summer schools in South Africa, exposing students to theoretical science. For the first time, they partnered with the Centre for High Performance Computing (CHPC) in 2019 and included streams on quantum computing and computational science.
This joint school sponsored students from around Africa with backgrounds in engineering, computer science, biology, informatics and statistics.
The second joint school in 2020 proved to be just as successful with a diverse audience and an even bigger focus on quantum computing with speakers from leading international organisations.

An excellent example was The Quantum Computing Summer School 2019 hosted by Wits University, run by their engineering department and included invited speakers from the University of KwaZulu-Natal (UKZN).
This joint school sponsored students from around Africa with backgrounds in engineering, computer science, biology, informatics and statistics.
